mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and weak consumer spending, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns exceed 55% of triggered instances, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 40% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle confirms robust economic growth that offsets higher rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25pp, or Rank IC turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC remains negative or below 0.01, or positive return frequency falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Market returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirm broad-based demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Combined positive signals produce negative forward returns in more than 45% of observations, or interaction effect vanishes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing contraction and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes fail to predict negative returns when UNRATE remains below 4.0%, or signal loses statistical significance.
